message:
wa-upg.10.5: schema validation + docs generation golden tests

Integration test suite validating the schema-driven pipeline:
- Schema file validation: valid JSON, required fields, $schema draft, $id
- additionalProperties enforcement (no field leaks)
- Registry ↔ disk coverage: no orphaned schemas, known gaps tracked
- Schema parsing: properties, types, required fields, $defs
- Docs generation determinism: same input → identical output
- Reference structure: header, TOC, categories, envelope, error codes
- Endpoint surface info (robot + MCP), experimental marking
- Summary table coverage, categorization distribution
- 28 integration tests loading real docs/json-schema/ files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -573,10 +573,10 @@
 {"id":"wa-upg.1.7","title":"Docs: incident bundles + wa reproduce (shareable support workflow)","description":"# Task: Document incident bundles and `wa reproduce`

## Goal
Make incident bundles discoverable and usable by humans and by support processes.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to run `wa reproduce`
  - what data is included by default (and what is not)
  - privacy implications + opt-in flags
  - how to replay a bundle
  - how to attach to a GitHub issue (or share internally)
- Include examples of:
  - a policy denial bundle
  - a rule replay bundle
  - a watcher crash bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new contributor can follow docs to generate and replay a bundle without guesswork.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10","title":"[EPIC] Schema-driven docs + client generation (robot/MCP as real API)","description":"# [EPIC] Schema-driven docs + client generation

## Mission
Treat wa's robot/MCP interfaces as a real API:
- schemas are authoritative
- docs are generated from schemas
- typed clients can be generated or maintained with confidence

## Why This Matters
If an integration breaks silently, users lose trust.
Schemas + generated docs/types provide:
- contract stability
- faster integration development
- safer refactors

## Scope
- Build on the existing JSON Schema work:
  - versioned schemas for robot outputs and error objects
- Add:
  - schema-driven docs pages (render schemas into readable reference)
  - optional client generation (at least one language) OR type-safe Rust client crate
  - CI checks to ensure schema/doc generation stays in sync

## Success Criteria
- Another tool can generate types and call wa robot/MCP reliably.
- Schema changes are intentional and reviewed.

## Testing
- Schema validations in CI.
- Golden tests for generated docs output stability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs reflect schemas automatically.
- At least one typed client path exists and is tested.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10.1","title":"Design: schema→docs/types strategy (direction, versioning, languages)","description":"# Task: Decide schema-driven generation strategy

## Goal
Choose the practical strategy for schema-driven docs and typed clients.

## Requirements
- Decide generation direction:
  - Rust structs → JSON Schema (preferred for single source of truth)
  - OR JSON Schema → types (and keep schemas authored by hand)
- Decide client target(s):
  - TypeScript (common for tooling)
  - Python (common for scripts)
  - Rust client crate (for in-repo safety)
- Decide versioning policy:
  - how schema versions map to wa versions
  - how breaking changes are detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Strategy is chosen and consistent with repo goals (robust, low tech debt).
","notes":"Decision documented in PLAN.md section 21.5; ready to close once parent unblocked.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:48:47.647300556Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:53:37.822413104Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-docs","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.10.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:44Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":226,"issue_id":"wa-upg.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Strategy decided: Rust structs → JSON Schema via schemars (single source of truth). Client priority: Rust P0, TypeScript P1, Python P2. Versioning: schema tracks wa semver, golden-file CI test for drift. Implemented ApiVersion (pre/post-1.0 compat), SchemaRegistry (24 endpoints), ChangeKind classification, SchemaDiffResult. 27 tests. Commit f15fcb4.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:53:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-upg.10.2","title":"Implement schema-driven docs generator (reference pages)","description":"# Task: Implement schema-driven docs generator

## Goal
Generate readable docs pages from the canonical schemas.

## Requirements
- Generate docs that include:
  - command list
  - request/response objects
  - error objects and codes
  - examples
- Output stability:
  - deterministic ordering
  - stable formatting (golden tests)

## Testing
- Golden tests for generated docs.
- CI check that generated output matches committed files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs pages are usable as an API reference without reading code.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10.3","title":"Typed client generation (at least one language) or Rust client crate","description":"# Task: Typed client generation / client crate

## Goal
Provide a typed client path so integrations are less brittle.

## Requirements
- Choose one practical deliverable:
  - TypeScript types + thin client wrappers
  - Python types + thin client wrappers
  - Rust client crate for wa robot/MCP
- Client must:
  - validate responses against schema (optional but recommended)
  - provide stable error handling (codes)

## Testing
- Integration test that uses the client to call a local wa process and validates schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A consumer can integrate with wa without hand-parsing JSON.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10.4","title":"CI: schema/doc/type generation drift checks","description":"# Task: CI drift checks for schema-driven outputs

## Goal
Ensure schemas, generated docs, and generated types never drift silently.

## Requirements
- CI step that:
  - regenerates docs/types
  - fails if repo has diffs
  - prints actionable diff hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Breaking schema changes are caught early.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10.6","title":"Integration: typed client calls wa robot and validates schemas","description":"# Task: Integration test for typed client

## Goal
Prove generated/typed clients work against a running wa instance and that schemas match reality.

## Requirements
- Stand up a local wa process (or fixture harness) and execute:
  - `wa robot state`
  - `wa robot events`
  - at least one command with errors
- Validate responses:
  - against JSON schemas
  - via typed client deserialization

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration test catches drift between wa outputs and schemas/types.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10.7","title":"Docs: integration guide (robot/MCP clients, schemas, versioning)","description":"# Task: Document integration guide

## Goal
Provide a practical guide for building integrations on wa robot/MCP.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - how to consume schemas
  - how to use generated types/clients
  - versioning and breaking change policy
  - troubleshooting common schema validation failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- A developer can build a simple integration with confidence.

 {"id":"wa-upg.11","title":"[EPIC] FTS explainability (why not searchable? indexing lag + errors)","description":"# [EPIC] FTS explainability

## Mission
Make search behavior legible by answering:
- "Why isn't this text searchable?"
- "Is indexing behind?"
- "Was this pane excluded?"

## Why This Matters
Search is only useful if users can trust it.
When it fails silently, users don't know if:
- data wasn't captured
- data was pruned
- indexing lagged
- pane excluded
- query invalid

## Scope
- Indexing progress and lag are visible.
- Search errors are actionable and explain *what to do next*.
- Provide an explain surface:
  - `wa query --explain` or `wa search explain <query/pane>`
  - robot equivalent

## Success Criteria
- Given a missing search result, wa can explain the most likely reason.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests for explain logic.
- E2E scenarios:
  - excluded pane
  - indexing lag
  - invalid FTS syntax

## Acceptance Criteria
- Explain output exists and is stable.
- Errors are actionable and include remediation.
